York Li
Projects Publications Experiences

Low-Burden Digital Phenotyping of Affective Risk: Positive Emoji Usage, Speech Rate, and Sleep Relate to College Student Mental Health

Under Review at Affective Science
25 March 2026 | Machine Learning Digital Phenotyping

Overview

Using the Meet Pandora smartphone app and tracking daily affect and passive behavioral markers in college students, we found that low-burden digital signals, specifically positive emoji usage, outperformed traditional questionnaires in predicting psychological flourishing and lower symptom burden.

My Role: Co-Author

  • Data Analysis: Developed R scripts to process and summarize participant metadata, contributing a clean summary for the study’s demographic profiling.

  • Review: Conducted technical reviews of the manuscript prior to journal submission.
